evals.prompt: /brainstorm i want to add dark mode to my app. it should look good and work on mobile.
evals.expected_output: Starts the interview because the prompt lacks scope boundaries (no non-goals), measurable constraints, and testable success criteria. Asks ONE question via AskUserQuestion. Does not propose approaches or write code yet.
expectations: Does NOT skip interview — prompt lacks all three skip criteria (scope boundaries, constraints, success criteria)
Asks exactly ONE question per turn via AskUserQuestion
Does NOT invoke implementation skills or write code before design is approved

evals.prompt: /brainstorm build a rate limiter for our API. non-goals: no distributed rate limiting, no Redis — in-memory only. constraints: p99 latency under 1ms, max 100 RPS per client IP. success: rate-limited responses return 429 with Retry-After header, passes load test at 200 RPS showing 50% rejection.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the prompt has scope boundaries (non-goals stated), measurable constraints (latency, RPS), and testable success criteria (429 + header, load test). May skip interview. If skipping, cites 2+ concrete details from the spec. Still goes through propose-approaches and present-design steps.
expectations: Recognizes all three skip criteria are met — may skip interview
If skipping, acknowledges by citing 2+ concrete details from the spec (not generic paraphrasing)
Still goes through design steps (propose approaches, present design) before any implementation

evals.prompt: /brainstorm --auto i need a plugin system for our CLI tool. users should be able to write plugins in JS or TS that hook into command lifecycle events.
evals.expected_output: With --auto, skips interview entirely and infers purpose/scope/constraints from prompt + project scan. Proposes 2-3 approaches with a clear recommendation. Auto-selects the recommended approach. Presents design sections without per-section confirmations. Stores final design in metadata.design.
expectations: Skips interview due to --auto flag — infers from prompt and project context
Proposes 2-3 approaches with a clear recommendation and justification
Auto-selects recommended approach and presents design without per-section user confirmations

evals.prompt:
/brainstorm i'm thinking about adding real-time collaboration to our markdown editor. like google docs but for markdown. users type and see each other's cursors.

after 2 interview questions, the user says: 'actually forget collab editing, i just want a share-via-link feature where others can view (read-only) the doc'
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the fundamental pivot from real-time collaboration to read-only sharing. Discards stale context about CRDTs/OT/cursors. Restarts from the relevant interview question for the new, much simpler scope.
expectations: Acknowledges the pivot and discards stale context about real-time editing
Restarts from a relevant question for the new scope (read-only sharing)
Does not carry forward complexity assumptions from the original collaboration feature
